herb_parts.name: root
herb_parts.desc: Angelica sinensis root, also known as dong quai, has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties. The root is rich in ferulic acid, ligustilide, and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it beneficial for relieving menstrual cramps and other symptoms associated with premenstrual syndrome. It is also used to treat conditions such as fever, cough, and digestive problems, and is believed to promote blood circulation and reduce blood pressure.

herb_parts.name: rhizome
herb_parts.desc: Angelica sinensis rhizome, also known as dang gui, is a medicinal part used in traditional Chinese medicine to promote blood circulation and alleviate symptoms of menstrual disorders. The rhizome is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce swelling and improve overall health. Its medicinal properties have been used to treat various conditions, including anemia, fatigue, and impaired immune function.

herb_parts.name: leaf
herb_parts.desc: Angelica sinensis leaf is primarily used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat various health issues, including fever and inflammation due to its anti-inflammatory properties. The leaf's flavonoids and phenolic acids contribute to its medicinal effects, such as reducing oxidative stress and improving cardiovascular health. Additionally, the leaf has been traditionally used to treat digestive problems and to reduce pain and swelling.

herb_parts.name: flower
herb_parts.desc: Angelica sinensis flower, also known as dong fang bo, is used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The flower is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, the flower is typically dried and used as an infusion or decoction to promote health and well-being.

herb_parts.name: seed
herb_parts.desc: Angelica sinensis seed is not commonly used as a medicinal part, but the seed oil is valued for its potential anti-inflammatory properties. However, it's worth noting that more research is needed to confirm the seed's medicinal properties and effects. Some traditional Chinese medicine sources suggest that the seed may have a role in treating fever and promoting digestion.

herb_parts.name: stem
herb_parts.desc: Angelica sinensis stem contains a rich source of ferulic acid and other bioactive compounds, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The stem is also a good source of volatile oils, including ligustilide and butylphthalide, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and anti-oxidative properties. These compounds have been found to play a role in reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health.

herb_parts.name: calyx
herb_parts.desc: Angelica sinensis calyx, also known as dong quai calyx, contains flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The calyx is traditionally used in Chinese medicine to treat menstrual disorders and promote blood circulation. Its medicinal properties are thought to be due to the presence of various bioactive compounds, including ferulic acid and sinensis acid.

herb_parts.name: petiole
herb_parts.desc: Angelica sinensis petiole, which is often referred to as the stem or stalk of the angelica plant, is used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties. The petiole is rich in flavonoids, saponins, and other bioactive compounds that have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. These compounds may contribute to the petiole's potential benefits in reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues.

herb_parts.name: stem tip
herb_parts.desc: Angelica sinensis stem tip contains volatile oils, phenolic acids, and polysaccharides, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive compounds present in the stem tip of Angelica sinensis may help in reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. Additionally, the stem tip is also rich in flavonoids and lignans, which have been found to possess hepatoprotective and immunomodulatory effects.

herb_parts.name: rootlet
herb_parts.desc: Angelica sinensis rootlet is primarily composed of ferulic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties and is used to treat various health conditions, including fever and rheumatism. The rootlet's sesquiterpenoids and coumarins also exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, contributing to its medicinal value. It is often used in traditional Chinese medicine to alleviate menstrual disorders and improve overall circulation.

herb_parts.name: inflorescence
herb_parts.desc: Angelica sinensis inflorescence is a traditional medicinal part used in Chinese medicine, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The inflorescence is believed to possess immunomodulatory and anti-anaphylactic effects, making it a potential treatment for various allergic reactions. Additionally, it is thought to have a protective effect on the liver and cardiovascular system.

herb_parts.name: peduncle
herb_parts.desc: Angelica sinensis peduncle is traditionally used in Chinese medicine, and it is believed to possess anti-inflammatory properties. The peduncle is also used to treat various conditions, including fever, cough, and sore throat, due to its purported ability to clear heat and relieve toxins. However, more research is needed to fully understand the medicinal properties and effects of Angelica sinensis peduncle on human health.

herb_parts.name: bract
herb_parts.desc: Angelica sinensis bract, the leafy green part of the plant, is used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and menstrual issues. The bract is rich in vitamins and minerals, such as vitamin C and potassium, which contribute to its medicinal properties. It is also used to promote digestion and relieve symptoms of colds and flu.

herb_parts.name: floral axis
herb_parts.desc: Angelica sinensis floral axis, also known as the flowering stalk, contains a compound called ferulic acid, which has been shown to have antioxidant properties and may help to reduce inflammation. The floral axis of Angelica sinensis also contains other bioactive compounds, such as volatile oils and sesquiterpenes, which may contribute to its medicinal effects. These compounds have been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to treat a range of health conditions, including digestive and respiratory problems.

herb_parts.name: filament
herb_parts.desc: Angelica sinensis filament, also known as dong quai, contains various medicinal compounds that contribute to its health benefits. The filament is rich in ferulic acid, which has antioxidant properties that help protect the body from damage caused by free radicals. Additionally, the filament's flavonoids and other bioactive compounds may help to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health.

herb_parts.name: anther
herb_parts.desc: Angelica sinensis anther, also known as dong quai anther, is a medicinal part used in traditional Chinese medicine. The anther of this plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of menstrual disorders and menopausal symptoms. It is often used in combination with other herbs to promote overall health and well-being.

herb_parts.name: style
herb_parts.desc: Angelica sinensis style refers to the rhizome and root of the plant, which are the medicinal parts commonly used in traditional Chinese medicine. These components are rich in bioactive compounds, including ferulic acid, ligustilide, and butylphthalide, which possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and vasodilatory properties. The rhizome and root of Angelica sinensis are traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and menstrual disorders.

herb_parts.name: stigma
herb_parts.desc: Angelica sinensis stigma, also known as the dried stigma of Angelica sinensis, is used in traditional Chinese medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects. The stigma of this plant is believed to have a regulating effect on the body's Qi and blood flow, which is thought to alleviate symptoms of menstrual disorders and other gynecological issues. In some traditional Asian medicinal practices, the stigma of Angelica sinensis is used to treat various health conditions, including menstrual irregularities and menopausal symptoms.
